description.en: Get the beginning or ending index of the completion scope. These indexes are the start and end arguments passed to the rl_attempted_completion_function callback of the underlying library. The values may be different in the same input editing scenario based on the underlying C readline implementation. Ex: libedit is known to behave differently than libreadline.

description.en: Get the completer function, or None if no completer function has been set.

excerpt.es: Get the completer function, or None if no completer function has been set.
excerpt.en: Get the completer function, or None if no completer function has been set.

description.en: Get the type of completion being attempted. This returns the rl_completion_type variable in the underlying library as an integer.

excerpt.es: Get the type of completion being attempted. This returns the rl_completion_type variable in the underlying library as an integer.
excerpt.en: Get the type of completion being attempted. This returns the rl_completion_type variable in the underlying library as an integer.

description.en: Return the number of items currently in the history. (This is different from get_history_length(), which returns the maximum number of lines that will be written to a history file.)

excerpt.es: Return the number of items currently in the history. (This is different from get_history_length(), which returns the maximum number of lines that will be written to a history file.)
excerpt.en: Return the number of items currently in the history. (This is different from get_history_length(), which returns the maximum number of lines that will be written to a history file.)

description.en: Return the current contents of history item at index. The item index is one-based. This calls history_get() in the underlying library.

excerpt.es: Return the current contents of history item at index. The item index is one-based. This calls history_get() in the underlying library.
excerpt.en: Return the current contents of history item at index. The item index is one-based. This calls history_get() in the underlying library.

description.en: Set or return the desired number of lines to save in the history file. The write_history_file() function uses this value to truncate the history file, by calling history_truncate_file() in the underlying library. Negative values imply unlimited history file size.

description.en: Return the current contents of the line buffer (rl_line_buffer in the underlying library).

excerpt.es: Return the current contents of the line buffer (rl_line_buffer in the underlying library).
excerpt.en: Return the current contents of the line buffer (rl_line_buffer in the underlying library).
